questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the fundamental rights of the citizens?
questions.explanation: It includes articles 12 to 35.

questions.question: According to the Panchayat Extension to Scheduled Areas Act, 1996 (PESA), which level of Panchayat is mandatory for Scheduled Areas in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Mandal Panchayat is mandatory for Scheduled Areas.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression?
questions.explanation: It ensures freedom of speech and expression.

questions.question: What is the name of the highest court in the state of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It is based in Kolkata.

questions.question: Under the Indian Constitution, which part deals with the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: It includes articles 36 to 51.

questions.question: Which of the following events marked the beginning of the Indian freedom struggle in Bengal?
questions.explanation: It was a highly contentious and divisive policy.

questions.question: Who was the prominent revolutionary involved in the Barisal Conspiracy Case of 1912?
questions.explanation: He was a key figure in the revolutionary movement.

questions.question: What was the name of the famous satyagraha led by Sarat Chandra Bose and other prominent Bengali leaders in 1941?
questions.explanation: It was a significant non-violent resistance campaign.

questions.question: Which of the following writers was associated with the influential Bengali literary magazine 'Dharmatattwa'?
questions.explanation: It was a leading Bengali literary publication.

questions.question: Who was the first Indian Governor of Bengal under British rule?
questions.explanation: He was the first Governor-General of India.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers originates in the Himalayas and flows through the Darjeeling district in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Tista River originates in the Himalayas.

questions.question: Which of the following national parks is located in the Sundarbans region of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Sundarbans National Park is located in the Sundarbans region.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain ranges runs parallel to the Siliguri Corridor in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Himalayan Mountains run parallel to the Siliguri Corridor.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest delta in the Indian subcontinent, located in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganges Delta is the largest delta in the Indian subcontinent.

questions.question: Which of the following states shares the longest international border with West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh shares the longest international border with West Bengal.

questions.question: The West Bengal government launched 'Daktar Khonje Kotha' to improve healthcare services in the state. What is the primary focus of this initiative?
questions.explanation: Daktar Khonje Kotha focuses on enhancing public awareness about health.

questions.question: The West Bengal government has set a target to increase the use of renewable energy in the state to what percentage by 2027?
questions.explanation: West Bengal aims to increase renewable energy use to 50% by 2027.

questions.question: The Kailash Chhetri-led West Bengal cricket team won the 2025-26 Ranji Trophy. Which city hosted the final match?
questions.explanation: The final match of the 2025-26 Ranji Trophy was hosted in Kolkata.

questions.question: The West Bengal government has implemented the 'Swasthya Sathi' scheme to provide health insurance to the state's citizens. What is the maximum family income limit for eligibility under this scheme?
questions.explanation: The maximum family income limit for eligibility under the Swasthya Sathi scheme is ₹ 5 lakhs.

questions.question: The West Bengal government has launched the 'Mukhya Mantri Karmatirtha Unnayan Yojana' to develop infrastructure in the state's industrial areas. Which of the following is a primary objective of this initiative?
options: To improve infrastructure in industrial areas
To encourage entrepreneurship in the state
questions.explanation: Mukhya Mantri Karmatirtha Unnayan Yojana aims to improve infrastructure in industrial areas.

questions.question: Which of the following is the objective of the 'Deshapriya' scheme launched by the Government of West Bengal?
options: To promote tourism in West Bengal
To provide financial assistance to entrepreneurs
To improve the education system of the state
To provide employment opportunities to the youth
questions.explanation: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to entrepreneurs.

questions.question: What is the main goal of the 'Kanyashree Prakalpa' scheme of the West Bengal government?
options: To provide free education to girls
To prevent child marriage and promote girls' education
To provide financial assistance to girls for higher education
To promote healthcare services for girls
questions.explanation: The scheme aims to prevent child marriage and promote girls' education.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is designed to provide financial assistance to the farmers of West Bengal for crop loss due to natural calamities?
questions.explanation: The scheme provides insurance coverage to the farmers.

questions.question: The 'Swasthya Sathi' scheme of the West Bengal government aims to provide
options: Free healthcare services to all citizens
Free insurance coverage to all citizens
Free food security to all citizens
questions.explanation: The scheme provides universal health insurance coverage.

questions.question: The 'Mukhya Mantri Swasthya Bima Yojana' of the West Bengal government provides
options: Free medical treatment to all citizens
Free medical treatment to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families
Free medical treatment to all patients suffering from serious diseases
Free medical treatment to all patients suffering from chronic diseases
questions.explanation: The scheme provides health insurance coverage to BPL families.

questions.question: A sum of ₹ 10,000 is invested for 3 years at 5% per annum compound interest. What will be the amount after 3 years?
questions.explanation: The correct amount will be calculated using the compound interest formula.

questions.question: If the simple interest on a certain sum of money for 2 years at 4% per annum is ₹ 160, then the principal amount is?
questions.explanation: The simple interest formula will be used to find the principal amount.

questions.question: What is the present value of an amount of ₹ 12,000 due in 5 years, if the rate of interest is 3% per annum?
questions.explanation: The present value formula for future amount will be used to find the answer.

questions.question: The compound interest on ₹ 5,000 at 8% per annum for 2 years is ₹ 840. What is the rate of interest for the next year?
questions.explanation: The compound interest formula will be used to find the rate of interest for the next year.

questions.question: The simple interest on a certain sum of money for 1 year at 6% per annum is ₹ 60. What will be the simple interest for 2 years?
questions.explanation: The simple interest formula will be used to find the simple interest for 2 years.

questions.question: A certain work can be completed by a group of workers in 10 days. If 2 workers are added to the group, in how many days will they complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Work done by the group remains the same.

questions.question: A water pipe can fill 2/3rd of a tank in 6 hours. How long will it take to fill the empty tank?
questions.explanation: It will take the same time to fill the entire tank.

questions.question: A group of 4 men can finish a work in 20 days. If 2 more men join the group, in how many days will the work be completed?
questions.explanation: Work done by the group remains the same.

questions.question: A tap can fill 2/3rd of a bucket in 24 minutes. How many minutes will it take to fill the bucket completely?
questions.explanation: It will take the same time to fill the entire bucket.

questions.question: A group of 8 workers can complete a certain work in 12 days. If 2 workers leave the group, in how many days will the remaining workers complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Work done by the group remains the same.

questions.question: In the given sequence: P, V, J, L, ?; which letter series continues?
questions.explanation: The sequence is based on alphabetical order.

questions.question: Replace the question mark with an appropriate letter in the given statement: 'Bengal's capital city is KOLKATA, and its ? is Howrah.'
questions.explanation: Howrah is the second city adjacent to Kolkata.

questions.question: If 5 is to 12 as 8 is to ?
questions.explanation: The pattern is obtained by multiplying the first number by a factor to get the second number.

questions.question: In the given statement: '-KOLKATA, being a city of rich culture, has a number of 'MELAS', 'FESTIVALS', 'FAIRS', and ?.'
questions.explanation: The statement lists various cultural events in Kolkata.

questions.question: In the given sequence: 4, 9, 16, 25, ?; which number series continues?
questions.explanation: The sequence is based on perfect squares of natural numbers.

questions.question: Kalyani is the husband of Sudarshan's sister. How is Kalyani related to Sudarshan?
questions.explanation: As she is the wife of his brother.

questions.question: Pritam is the son of Rohan's mother's brother. What is the relationship between Pritam and Rohan?
questions.explanation: As he is the son of his mother's brother.

questions.question: Amit's mother is the sister of Bapi's father. What is the relationship between Amit and Bapi?
questions.explanation: As his mother is the sister of his father.

questions.question: Dilip is the son of Subhas's wife's sister. What is the relationship between Dilip and Subhas?
questions.explanation: As he is the son of his wife's sister.

questions.question: Sneha is the daughter of Chandan's father's sister. What is the relationship between Sneha and Chandan?
questions.explanation: As she is the daughter of his father's sister.
